name: shadcn MCP Server
description: MCP server built into the shadcn CLI (run via npx shadcn@latest mcp) that lets AI agents browse, search, and install UI components from any shadcn-compatible registry, including private registries, using @registry/name namespacing. Shipped with CLI 3.0 in August 2025.

evidence.value: Namespaced @registry/name resolution reliably targets components across configured registries
Installs reuse the mature shadcn add pipeline, handling dependencies, file placement, and framework detection
Browse and search tools surface components with metadata from any shadcn-compatible registry

component_search_quality.methodology: Search relevance assessment over public registries

evidence.value: CLI surfaces clear errors for missing registries, unreachable URLs, and dependency conflicts; partial installs may need manual cleanup

error_recovery.methodology: Failure mode and error message testing

evidence.value: Installs source code from any configured shadcn-compatible registry directly into the project; third-party registries are unvetted and the key risk surface

supply_chain_risk.methodology: Supply chain threat modeling of registry-sourced code installation

evidence.value: Agent-initiated installs write component files and add npm dependencies; review depends on the MCP client's approval flow and code review practices

code_installation_control.methodology: Write-action control and approval flow analysis

evidence.value: Private registries supported with token-based authentication configured via environment variables in components.json
Registry tokens read from local environment; no credentials sent to third parties beyond the configured registry endpoints
An agent can install components (and transitively their dependencies) into the codebase; a malicious or typosquatted registry entry executes at build/runtime

unauthorized_action_risk.methodology: Blast radius assessment of install actions

evidence.value: Server only fetches public or configured registry metadata and component source; it does not read or transmit project code externally

data_exposure.methodology: Data flow analysis of registry requests

evidence.value: Minimal sensitive data involved; main consideration is keeping private registry tokens out of committed configuration
Search and install requests are visible to whichever registries are configured, including third-party ones

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Outbound request and data sharing review

evidence.value: Runs locally over stdio via npx shadcn@latest mcp with no hosted service or telemetry pipeline collecting project data
Clear official docs covering MCP setup per client, registry configuration, and namespacing, plus a detailed CLI 3.0 changelog
Installed files and dependency changes are fully visible in the project diff; tool calls appear in MCP client logs
MIT-licensed; MCP server ships as part of the open shadcn-ui/ui monorepo and CLI
Tool surface is small and well-defined: browse, search, and install components from configured registries
One-line stdio config (npx shadcn@latest mcp) with documented one-command setup for major MCP clients
Lightweight local process; latency dominated by registry HTTP fetches and npm dependency installs
Built on the actively maintained shadcn CLI with frequent releases since the August 2025 3.0 launch
Covers discovery and installation across public and private shadcn-compatible registries; scoped to UI components rather than general tooling

feature_coverage.methodology: Feature completeness assessment within its domain

evidence.value: shadcn/ui is one of the most-starred UI projects on GitHub and the MCP server is the standard agent integration for its ecosystem

strengths: First-party integration with the dominant shadcn/ui component ecosystem
Browse, search, and install from any shadcn-compatible registry, including private ones
Runs locally over stdio with no hosted service and minimal data exposure
Trivial setup via npx shadcn@latest mcp with per-client docs
All installed code lands in the project diff for review
MIT-licensed and developed in the open shadcn-ui/ui monorepo
limitations: Installs third-party source code and npm dependencies into the project — registry supply-chain risk is the key concern
No built-in vetting or signing of third-party registry components
Agent-initiated installs depend on the MCP client's approval flow for human oversight
Scope limited to UI component workflows in shadcn-compatible projects
Private registry tokens must be carefully kept out of committed config

metadata.authentication: Optional registry tokens for private registries

code-generation.notes: Excellent for AI-assisted frontend development: agents can find and install real, vetted-by-usage components instead of hallucinating UI code

content-creation.notes: Useful when building landing pages and content sites on shadcn-based stacks

education.notes: Good for teaching modern React/Tailwind component patterns with installable examples

research-assistant.notes: Limited to exploring component registries; not a general research tool

data-analysis.notes: Only relevant for installing chart and dashboard UI components, not for analysis itself

best_for: Frontend teams building with shadcn/ui and Tailwind who want agent-driven component installs
Organizations distributing internal design systems via private shadcn registries
AI coding agents that should use real registry components rather than generated approximations
